title: Tragacantha douglasii

Within the kingdom Plantae, Tragacantha douglasii is a member of the phylum Magnoliophyta and the class Magnoliopsida.

intro: Tragacantha douglasii, also known as Douglas's needle grass or squirreltail, is a perennial grass native to North America, belonging to the family Poaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Tragacantha douglasii include sesquiterpene lactones and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Tragacantha douglasii is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, often used to treat digestive complaints and skin conditions.
